paper_title: GPT-NeoX-20B: An Open-Source Autoregressive Language Model

raw_extraction.innovation: Introduces a 20B-parameter autoregressive language model trained on The Pile, leveraging Rotary Positional Embeddings (Su et al., 2021) applied selectively to the first 25% of embedding dimensions, alongside optimized training techniques like parallel attention computation and exclusive dense layers for improved efficiency
raw_extraction.pretraining_corpus: The Pile (CommonCore, OpenWebText2, StackExchange, Prose, Dialogue, Miscellaneous)
raw_extraction.application: General-purpose language understanding, mathematical reasoning, knowledge-based tasks, and few-shot learning
raw_extraction.research_problem: Scaling large language models with open-source accessibility and transparency
raw_extraction.hardware_used: Twelve Supermicro AS-4124 GO-NART servers with eight NVIDIA A100-SXM4-40GB GPUs, InfiniBand network, and distributed training with ZeRO optimizer
raw_extraction.extension: Rotary positional embeddings applied only to the first 25% of embedding dimensions, exclusive dense layers, and parallel attention computation for efficiency
